revision.text:
[[de:Ab-Schetui]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = D36:D58 I2-Z2:X1 N14-N14-N33*N33:N33-HASH |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = |NAME-EXPLANATION = Ab-Schetui
''ˁb-Štw.t'' -(reads: ''ˁb-Št.wj'')
''Cleansing of the Turtles'' }} '''Ab-Schetui''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|qid=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt|1=Ancient Egypt}} {{ill|Constellation|en|Constellation|qid=Q8928|lt=constellation|1=Constellation}} that comprised at least four {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Dekan-Sterne|ja|バクティウ|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and included the two {{ill|decan (Ancient Egypt)|de|Einzeldekane|ja|古代エジプトのデカン|1=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decan (Ancient Egypt)|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|13=User:Immanuelle/User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decan (Ancient Egypt)|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/decan (Ancient Egypt)|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|Ab (decan)|de|Ab|ja|アブ（デカン）|lt=Ab|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ab (decan)|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ab (decan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ab (decan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Schetui|de|Schetui|ja|シェトゥイ|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schetui|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schetui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schetui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Background == === Ab-Schetui as a Constellation === [[File:Ab-Schetui.PNG|thumb|Constellation Schetui in {{ill|Egyptian hieroglyphs|en|Egyptian hieroglyphs|qid=Q132659|lt=Egyptian Hieroglyphs|1=Egyptian hieroglyphs}} (Temple {{ill|Ramesses II|en|Ramesses II|qid=Q1523|lt=Ramses II|1=Ramesses II}}, {{ill|Thebes (Ancient Egypt)|de|Theben (Altes Ägypten)|ja|テーベ（古代エジプト）|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thebes (Ancient Egypt)|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thebes (Ancient Egypt)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Thebes (Ancient Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})]] Ab-Schetui is equated with the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Turtle|en|Turtle|qid=Q223044|lt=Turtles|1=Turtle}} and the modern constellation {{ill|Cancer (constellation)|en|Cancer (constellation)|qid=Q8849|lt=Cancer (constellation)|1=Cancer (constellation)}}. In the decan lists of {{ill|Nutbook#Pharaonic Period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|ja|ナトブック#ファラオ時代|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nutbook#Pharaonic Period|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nutbook#Pharaonic Period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nutbook#Pharaonic Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Book of Nut|en|Book of Nut|qid=Q2005559|lt=Nutbook|1=Book of Nut}}, the decan associated with the constellation Ab-Schetui, {{ill|Ab (decan)|de|Ab|ja|アブ（デカン）|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ab (decan)|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ab (decan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ab (decan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is not mentioned with astronomical data, even though Ab appears in the depiction of the goddess {{ill|Nut|en|Nut|qid=Q1265417|lt=Nut (Egyptian mythology)|1=Nut}} at the bottom of the image and, together with the first decan {{ill|Kenmet (decan)|de|Kenmut|ja|ケンムト（デカン）|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenmet (decan)|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenmet (decan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenmet (decan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, inaugurates the new calendar year at {{ill|Schemu#Datings|de|Schemu#Datierungen|ja|シェムウ#日付|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schemu#Datings|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schemu#Datings|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Schemu#Datings|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The associated {{ill|heliacal|de|heliakal|ja|ヘリアカル|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/heliacal|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/heliacal|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/heliacal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} rising symbolized rebirth. Schetui, as the 36th decan, had its heliacal rising 15 days earlier, on {{ill|Season of the Emergence|en|Season of the Emergence|qid=Q2037143|lt=26 Peret IV|1=Season of the Emergence}}. The dating was based on the established order under {{ill|Sesostris III|de|Sesostris III|ja|セソストリスIII世|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sesostris III|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sesostris III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sesostris III|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|qid=Q719639|lt=12th Dynasty|1=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt}}) in his seventh year of reign. === Ab-Schetui in Ancient Egyptian Mythology === The turtle was counted among the companions of {{ill|Set (deity)|en|Set (deity)|qid=Q131795|lt=Seth (Egyptian mythology)|1=Set (deity)}} and additionally possessed the {{ill|iconographic|de|ikonografisch|ja|イコノグラフィー|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iconographic|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iconographic|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iconographic|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} attribute of the deity {{ill|Apophis (disambiguation)|en|Apophis (disambiguation)|qid=Q396228|lt=Apophis|1=Apophis (disambiguation)}}. Thus, the turtle belonged to the "enemies of {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|qid=Q1252904|lt=Re (Egyptian mythology)|1=Ra}}". In the {{ill|Neith (disambiguation)|en|Neith (disambiguation)|qid=Q436365|lt=Neith (Egyptian mythology)|1=Neith (disambiguation)}}-{{ill|Cosmogony|en|Cosmogony|qid=Q170835|lt=Cosmogony|1=Cosmogony}}, Re and Apophis are referred to as brothers. Further descriptions show interesting parallels to the {{ill|Osiris myth|de|Osiris-Mythos|ja|オシリス神話|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osiris myth|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osiris myth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osiris myth|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In various legends, Apophis is killed by numerous gods, only to repeatedly come back to life as a symbol of "rebirth". In this context, the epithet of the turtle decan ''{{ill|Ab (decan)|de|Ab|ja|アブ（デカン）|lt=Ab-anch-schetu|lt_de=Ab-anch-schetu|lt_ja=アブ・アンチ・シェトゥイ|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ab (decan)|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ab (decan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ab (decan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' ("Cleansing of the Turtle's Life") becomes understandable, as it likewise embodied the death and rebirth of the {{ill|Nile|en|Nile|qid=Q3392|lt=Nile|1=Nile}} with regard to the {{ill|Nile flood|de|Nilflut|ja|ナイル氾濫|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nile flood|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nile flood|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nile flood|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Edouard Naville: ''The Egyptian Book of the Dead of the 18th to 20th Dynasties from Various Documents.'' Academic Printing and Publishing House, Graz 1971 (reprint of the Berlin 1886 edition), saying 161. The descriptions in the solar hymns reveal how Apophis is dismembered with knives or stabbed with spears. His blood stains the sky red at sunrise. The {{ill|Solar deity|en|Solar deity|qid=Q511056|lt=sun god|1=Solar deity}} Re could rise in the sky as {{ill|Chepre|de|Chepri|ja|チェプリ|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chepre|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chepre|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chepre|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} after the death of Apophis or, respectively, the death of the turtle. This moment was reflected by the constellation Ab-Schetui. In the {{ill|Papyrus Carlsberg 1|de|Papyrus Carlsberg 1|ja|カルスバーグパピルス1|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Carlsberg 1|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Carlsberg 1|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Carlsberg 1|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the important role is explicitly commented: {{Zitat|The rising of {{ill|Kenmet (decan)|de|Kenmut|ja|ケンムト（デカン）|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenmet (decan)|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenmet (decan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenmet (decan)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} together with Ab-Schetui is the life of {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|qid=Q84122|lt=Horus|1=Horus}}; that is, the place where Kenmut rises on the ''{{ill|Nile flood|de|Nilflut|ja|ナイル氾濫|lt=High Day|lt_de=Hoher Tag|lt_ja=ハイデイ|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nile flood|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nile flood|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nile flood|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' is the place where Ab rises. The place where Schetui rises on the ''High Day'' is the place where Re rises. That is the rising of Re; that is: the location of the rising, which Kenmut makes together with Ab-Schetui.|Line 15 from Papyrus Carlsberg 1Alexandra von Lieven: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so-called Nutbook.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, p. 52.|qq=}} In further hymns from {{ill|Abydos (Ancient Egypt)|de|Abydos (Altes Ägypten)|ja|アビドス（古代エジプト）|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abydos (Ancient Egypt)|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abydos (Ancient Egypt)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abydos (Ancient Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the turtle was counted among the "those waiting in the retinue of {{ill|Set (deity)|en|Set (deity)|qid=Q131795|lt=Seth (Egyptian mythology)|1=Set (deity)}}", who would drink in the entire Nile should Seth succeed in penetrating "the Land of Light in the {{ill|Duat|en|Duat|qid=Q871389|lt=Duat|1=Duat}}". As part of the mythological New Year festival ''{{ill|Sothis Festival|de|Sothis-Fest|ja|ソティス祭|lt=Birth of Sothis|lt_de=Geburt der Sothis|lt_ja=ソティスの誕生|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothis Festival|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothis Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sothis Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', the Egyptians jubilantly and relievedly celebrated death and rebirth at the time of the Nile flood: "The turtle is dead, Re lives, the turtle is dead". == Literature == * Christian Leitz: ''Ancient Egyptian Star Clocks.'' Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * Siegfried Schott: ''Ancient Egyptian Festal Dates.'' Publishing House of the Academy of Sciences and Literature, Mainz / Wiesbaden 1950, pp. 12–13. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|ja|アレクサンドラ・フォン・リーヴェン|12=simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|13=User:Immanuelle/simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Outline of the Course of the Stars – The so-called Nutbook.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and p. 385. == References == {{translated page|de|Ab-Schetui|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Ab-Schetui]] {{wikidata link|Q303840}}
